steps.title: Identify the modifier rule
steps.content: The sentence opens with a participial phrase: "Wanting to celebrate the 100th anniversary of the Alaska Purchase." A participial phrase at the start of a sentence must logically modify the grammatical subject of the clause that follows the comma. Whatever noun comes right after the comma must be capable of "wanting" to celebrate.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'a contest sponsored by the Alaska Centennial Commission would award $300...': A contest cannot want to celebrate an anniversary — this creates a dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice B — 'an award of $300 would go to an individual...': An award cannot want to celebrate — another dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice C — '$300 would be awarded to an individual...': $300 cannot want to celebrate — dangling modifier again. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the Alaska Centennial Commission sponsored a contest...': The Alaska Centennial Commission is the entity that logically wanted to celebrate, so placing it as the subject immediately after the modifying phrase is correct. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
